description: A structured team builds something new from a brief: architect, builders, and a critic collaborate, then an editor ships it.

Unlike `refine` (a uniform improver relay on an existing thing), this is a differentiated team creating from scratch. Staff `architect` (shapes the approach), `builder` (produces the work — one or more), `critic` (pressure-tests), optionally `researcher` (facts / prior art), and `editor` (final cut). Sequential, so each voice builds on the last. Use rounds >= 2.

roles.description: Shape the overall approach and structure before anyone builds. Set direction, don't produce the final work.
Produce the actual work along the architect's direction. Be concrete and complete.
Pressure-test what's been built: gaps, risks, weak spots. Be specific and constructive.
Supply relevant facts, prior art, or constraints the team is missing. Cite specifics.
Cut the collaboration down to one clean, shippable final version. Keep the best, drop the rest.
